WHO Immunization Implementation Guide
0.2.0 - ci-build

  • Table of Contents

WHO Immunization Implementation Guide, published by WHO. This guide is not an authorized publication; it is the continuous build for version 0.2.0 built by the FHIR (HL7® FHIR® Standard) CI Build. This version is based on the current content of https://github.com/WorldHealthOrganization/smart-immunizations/tree/116/merge and changes regularly. See the Directory of published versions

5.1 Artifact Index
5.1.1 Decision Table Output Assess if supplementary booster TBE dose(s) are necessary and create a clinical note.
5.1.2 Decision Table Output BCG immunization schedule is complete
5.1.3 Decision Table Output BCG immunization schedule is complete. One BCG primary series dose was administered.
5.1.4 Decision Table Output BCG vaccination could be contraindicated. Clinical judgement is required. Create a clinical note.
5.1.5 Decision Table Output BCG vaccination is contraindicated
5.1.6 Decision Table Output Booster series is complete
5.1.7 Decision Table Output Client has acute gastroenteritis, consider risks of vaccination and make a judgement. Consider delaying rotavirus vaccination.
5.1.8 Decision Table Output Client has altered immunocompetence other than severe combined immunodeficiency, consider risks of vaccination and make a judgement.
5.1.9 Decision Table Output Client has bladder exstrophy, consider risks of vaccination and make a judgement.
5.1.10 Decision Table Output Client has chronic gastrointestinal disease, consider risks of vaccination and make a judgement.
5.1.11 Decision Table Output Client has moderate to severe fever, consider risks of vaccination and make a judgement. Consider delaying rotavirus vaccination.
5.1.12 Decision Table Output Client has spina bifida, consider risks of vaccination and make a judgement.
5.1.13 Decision Table Output Client has symptomatic HIV infection. Measles vaccination may be considered if the client is not severely immunosuppressed according to conventional definitions, consider risks of vaccination and make a clinical judgement.
5.1.14 Decision Table Output Client is due for a hepatitis B vaccination
5.1.15 Decision Table Output Client is due for BCG vaccination
5.1.16 Decision Table Output Client is due for bOPV and IPV vaccination
5.1.17 Decision Table Output Client is due for bOPV vaccination
5.1.18 Decision Table Output Client is due for cholera booster dose
5.1.19 Decision Table Output Client is due for cholera vaccination
5.1.20 Decision Table Output Client is due for dengue vaccination
5.1.21 Decision Table Output Client is due for DTP vaccination
5.1.22 Decision Table Output Client is due for Hepatitis A vaccination
5.1.23 Decision Table Output Client is due for hepatitis B monovalent vaccine
5.1.24 Decision Table Output Client is due for hepatitis B vaccination
5.1.25 Decision Table Output Client is due for Hib vaccination
5.1.26 Decision Table Output Client is due for HPV vaccination
5.1.27 Decision Table Output Client is due for IPV vaccination
5.1.28 Decision Table Output Client is due for JE vaccination
5.1.29 Decision Table Output Client is due for malaria vaccination
5.1.30 Decision Table Output Client is due for MCV1
5.1.31 Decision Table Output Client is due for MCV2
5.1.32 Decision Table Output Client is due for meningococcal vaccination
5.1.33 Decision Table Output Client is due for mumps vaccination
5.1.34 Decision Table Output Client is due for pneumococcal booster dose
5.1.35 Decision Table Output Client is due for pneumococcal vaccination
5.1.36 Decision Table Output Client is due for rabies vaccination
5.1.37 Decision Table Output Client is due for rotavirus vaccination
5.1.38 Decision Table Output Client is due for rubella vaccination
5.1.39 Decision Table Output Client is due for seasonal influenza vaccination
5.1.40 Decision Table Output Client is due for TBE vaccination
5.1.41 Decision Table Output Client is due for tetanus and diphtheria booster dose
5.1.42 Decision Table Output Client is due for the bOPV birth dose
5.1.43 Decision Table Output Client is due for typhoid booster dose
5.1.44 Decision Table Output Client is due for Typhoid vaccination
5.1.45 Decision Table Output Client is due for varicella vaccination
5.1.46 Decision Table Output Client is due for yellow fever vaccination
5.1.47 Decision Table Output Client is immunocompromised, consider risks of vaccination and make a judgement. Consider 2 dose schedule.
5.1.48 Decision Table Output Client is not due for a hepatitis B vaccination
5.1.49 Decision Table Output Client is not due for BCG vaccination
5.1.50 Decision Table Output Client is not due for bOPV vaccination
5.1.51 Decision Table Output Client is not due for cholera booster dose
5.1.52 Decision Table Output Client is not due for cholera vaccination
5.1.53 Decision Table Output Client is not due for dengue vaccination
5.1.54 Decision Table Output Client is not due for DTP vaccination
5.1.55 Decision Table Output Client is not due for first dose of measles-containing vaccine (MCV1)
5.1.56 Decision Table Output Client is not due for Hepatitis A vaccination
5.1.57 Decision Table Output Client is not due for Hib vaccination
5.1.58 Decision Table Output Client is not due for HPV vaccination
5.1.59 Decision Table Output Client is not due for IPV vaccination
5.1.60 Decision Table Output Client is not due for JE vaccination
5.1.61 Decision Table Output Client is not due for malaria vaccination
5.1.62 Decision Table Output Client is not due for MCV0
5.1.63 Decision Table Output Client is not due for MCV1
5.1.64 Decision Table Output Client is not due for MCV2
5.1.65 Decision Table Output Client is not due for measles supplementary dose
5.1.66 Decision Table Output Client is not due for meningococcal vaccination
5.1.67 Decision Table Output Client is not due for mumps vaccination
5.1.68 Decision Table Output Client is not due for pertussis booster dose
5.1.69 Decision Table Output Client is not due for pneumococcal booster dose
5.1.70 Decision Table Output Client is not due for pneumococcal vaccination
5.1.71 Decision Table Output Client is not due for polio vaccination
5.1.72 Decision Table Output Client is not due for rabies vaccination
5.1.73 Decision Table Output Client is not due for rotavirus vaccination
5.1.74 Decision Table Output Client is not due for rotavirus vaccination if immunization schedule is not complete.
5.1.75 Decision Table Output Client is not due for rubella vaccination
5.1.76 Decision Table Output Client is not due for seasonal influenza vaccination
5.1.77 Decision Table Output Client is not due for second dose of measles-containing vaccine (MCV2)
5.1.78 Decision Table Output Client is not due for TBE vaccination
5.1.79 Decision Table Output Client is not due for tetanus and diphtheria booster dose
5.1.80 Decision Table Output Client is not due for the bOPV birth dose
5.1.81 Decision Table Output Client is not due for the hepatitis B birth dose
5.1.82 Decision Table Output Client is not due for typhoid booster dose
5.1.83 Decision Table Output Client is not due for Typhoid vaccination
5.1.84 Decision Table Output Client is not due for varicella vaccination
5.1.85 Decision Table Output Client is not due for yellow fever vaccination
5.1.86 Decision Table Output Client is receiving or has received medications that may be immunosuppressive, consider risks of vaccination and make a judgement.
5.1.87 Decision Table Output Client should follow delayed or interrupted series decision logic
5.1.88 Decision Table Output Client should follow delayed-start decision logic
5.1.89 Decision Table Output Client should follow pregnancy starting with 3 doses decision logic
5.1.90 Decision Table Output Client should follow pregnancy starting with 4 doses decision logic
5.1.91 Decision Table Output Clinical judgement is required.
5.1.92 Decision Table Output Clinical judgement is required. Create a clinical note.
5.1.93 Decision Table Output Clinical judgement is required. Create clinical note
5.1.94 Decision Table Output Clinical judgement required. Create a clinical note
5.1.95 Decision Table Output Consider MCV0.
5.1.96 Decision Table Output Consider measles supplementary dose. Create a clinical note
5.1.97 Decision Table Output Consider postponing the vaccination if the client has fever > 38.5°C or other signs of serious disease. Consider risks of vaccination and make a clinical judgement.
5.1.98 Decision Table Output Dengue immunization schedule is complete
5.1.99 Decision Table Output Dengue immunization schedule is complete. Three dengue primary series doses were administered.
5.1.100 Decision Table Output Dengue vaccination could be contraindicated. Clinical judgement is required. Create a clinical note.
5.1.101 Decision Table Output Dengue vaccination is contraindicated
5.1.102 Decision Table Output Discuss pregnancy intentions with client, consider risks of vaccination and make a clinical judgement. Clients planning a pregnancy are advised to avoid pregnancy for 1 month after rubella vaccination.
5.1.103 Decision Table Output Discuss pregnancy intentions, consider risks of vaccination and make a clinical judgement. It is recommended to delay pregnancy for 4 weeks after vaccination.
5.1.104 Decision Table Output Do not vaccinate client with BCG as BCG vaccination is contraindicated for clients undergoing immunosuppressive treatment
5.1.105 Decision Table Output Do not vaccinate client with BCG as BCG vaccination is contraindicated for clients with immunodeficiency syndromes
5.1.106 Decision Table Output Do not vaccinate client with BCG if client is exposed to or receives immunosuppressive treatment
5.1.107 Decision Table Output Do not vaccinate client with BCG if the client had previous allergic reaction to any component of the vaccine
5.1.108 Decision Table Output Do not vaccinate client with dengue as dengue vaccination is contraindicated for lactating women.
5.1.109 Decision Table Output Do not vaccinate client with dengue as dengue vaccination is contraindicated for pregnant women.
5.1.110 Decision Table Output Do not vaccinate client with dengue as dengue vaccination is contraindicated in immunocompromised individuals
5.1.111 Decision Table Output Do not vaccinate client with dengue as dengue vaccination is contraindicated in individuals with symptomatic HIV.
5.1.112 Decision Table Output Do not vaccinate client with dengue as dengue vaccination is contraindicated in severely immunosuppresed individuals.
5.1.113 Decision Table Output Do not vaccinate client with dengue if client has had a severe allergic reaction to any component of the dengue vaccine or after prior administration of the dengue vaccine.
5.1.114 Decision Table Output Do not vaccinate client with hepatitis A as hepatitis A vaccination is contraindicated for severely immunocompromised clients.
5.1.115 Decision Table Output Do not vaccinate client with HPV if client has history of a severe allergic reaction after a previous HPV vaccine dose, or to a component of the vaccine.
5.1.116 Decision Table Output Do not vaccinate client with inactivated hepatitis A vaccination if client has had a severe allergic reaction to the previous dose.
5.1.117 Decision Table Output Do not vaccinate client with measles as measles vaccination is contraindicated for severely immunosuppressed client.
5.1.118 Decision Table Output Do not vaccinate client with measles if client has history of anaphylactic reaction to any component of the vaccine (e.g. neomycin or gelatin).
5.1.119 Decision Table Output Do not vaccinate client with measles if client has history of severe allergic reactions to any component of the vaccine (e.g. neomycin or gelatin).
5.1.120 Decision Table Output Do not vaccinate client with mumps as mumps vaccination is contraindicated in immunosuppressed individuals.
5.1.121 Decision Table Output Do not vaccinate client with mumps as mumps vaccination is contraindicated in individuals with immunodeficiency syndromes.
5.1.122 Decision Table Output Do not vaccinate client with mumps if client has allergy to vaccine components, such as neomycin and gelatin.
5.1.123 Decision Table Output Do not vaccinate client with rotavirus as rotavirus vaccincation is contraindicated for clients with severe immunodeficiency, including severe combined immunodeficiency.
5.1.124 Decision Table Output Do not vaccinate client with rotavirus if client had severe allergic reactions to a previous rotavirus vaccine dose or rotavirus vaccine components.
5.1.125 Decision Table Output Do not vaccinate client with rotavirus if client has history of anaphylactic reaction to a previous rotavirus vaccine dose or rotavirus vaccine components.
5.1.126 Decision Table Output Do not vaccinate client with rubella as rubella vaccination is contraindicated for clients receiving or exposed to immunosuppressive therapy.
5.1.127 Decision Table Output Do not vaccinate client with rubella as rubella vaccination is contraindicated for clients with active TB.
5.1.128 Decision Table Output Do not vaccinate client with rubella as rubella vaccination is contraindicated for clients with severe immunodeficiency.
5.1.129 Decision Table Output Do not vaccinate client with rubella as rubella vaccination is contraindicated for clients with symptomatic HIV infection.
5.1.130 Decision Table Output Do not vaccinate client with rubella as rubella vaccination is contraindicated until at least 3 months after stopping receiving blood products.
5.1.131 Decision Table Output Do not vaccinate client with rubella if client has experienced a severe allergic reaction after a previous vaccine dose or vaccine component.
5.1.132 Decision Table Output Do not vaccinate client with seasonal influenza if client has had a severe allergic reaction (e.g. anaphylaxis) after a previous dose or to a vaccine component.
5.1.133 Decision Table Output Do not vaccinate client with tetanus as tetanus vaccination is contraindicated for clients with severe acute illness
5.1.134 Decision Table Output Do not vaccinate for dengue if client has had a severe allergic reaction to any component of the dengue vaccine or after prior administration of the dengue vaccine.
5.1.135 Decision Table Output For bOPV dose, do not vaccinate client for bOPV dose as bOPV vaccination is contranindicated in clients with immunodeficiency disorders.
5.1.136 Decision Table Output For bOPV dose, do not vaccinate client for bOPV dose as bOPV vaccination is contranindicated in severely immunocompromised clients.
5.1.137 Decision Table Output Hepatitis A immunization schedule is complete
5.1.138 Decision Table Output Hepatitis A immunization schedule is complete. One hepatitis A primary series dose was administered.
5.1.139 Decision Table Output Hepatitis A immunization schedule is complete. Two hepatitis A primary series doses were administered.
5.1.140 Decision Table Output Hepatitis A vaccination could be contraindicated. Clinical judgement is required. Create a clinical note
5.1.141 Decision Table Output Hepatitis B birth dose was already administered. Check hepatitis B immunization schedule.
5.1.142 Decision Table Output Hepatitis B immunization schedule is complete
5.1.143 Decision Table Output Hepatitis B immunization schedule is complete for client whose weight at birth was over 2000 g and who was not premature infant. Two hepatitis B primary series doses were administered.
5.1.144 Decision Table Output Hepatitis B immunization schedule is complete. Three hepatitis B primary series doses were administered.
5.1.145 Decision Table Output Hib immunization schedule is complete
5.1.146 Decision Table Output Hib immunization schedule is complete as clients age was more than 1 year when first Hib dose was administered.
5.1.147 Decision Table Output Hib immunization schedule is complete as clients age was more than 1 year when first Hib dose was administered. One Hib primary series dose was administered.
5.1.148 Decision Table Output Hib immunization schedule is complete. Three Hib primary series doses and a booster dose were administered.
5.1.149 Decision Table Output Hib immunization schedule is complete. Three Hib primary series doses and one booster dose have been administered.
5.1.150 Decision Table Output Hib immunization schedule is complete. Three Hib primary series doses were administered.
5.1.151 Decision Table Output HPV immunization schedule is complete
5.1.152 Decision Table Output HPV immunization schedule is complete. One HPV primary series dose was administered.
5.1.153 Decision Table Output HPV immunization schedule is complete. Three HPV primary series doses were administered.
5.1.154 Decision Table Output HPV immunization schedule is complete. Two HPV primary series doses were administered.
5.1.155 Decision Table Output HPV vaccination could be contraindicated. Clinical judgement is required. Create a clinical note
5.1.156 Decision Table Output JE immunization schedule is complete
5.1.157 Decision Table Output JE immunization schedule is complete. One JE primary series dose was administered.
5.1.158 Decision Table Output JE immunization schedule is complete. One JE primary series dose was administered. Check for any other vaccines due.
5.1.159 Decision Table Output JE immunization schedule is complete. Two JE primary series doses were administered.
5.1.160 Decision Table Output Live attentuated Hepatitis A-containing vaccine is contraindicated
5.1.161 Decision Table Output Malaria immunization schedule is complete
5.1.162 Decision Table Output Malaria immunization schedule is complete. Four malaria primary series doses were administered.
5.1.163 Decision Table Output May administer meningococcal booster dose if the client is considered to be at continued high risk of exposure. Evaluate the client and use clinical judgement.
5.1.164 Decision Table Output May vaccinate client with MCV0 as client is within appropriate age range, MCV0 was not administered and no live vaccine was administered in the past 4 weeks. Check if one of the MCV0 specific scenarios is applicable.
5.1.165 Decision Table Output May vaccinate client with measles supplementary dose as supplementary dose was not administered, measles routine immunization schedule is complete and no live vaccine administered in the past 4 weeks.
5.1.166 Decision Table Output MCV0 was administered.
5.1.167 Decision Table Output Measles immunization schedule is complete
5.1.168 Decision Table Output Measles immunization schedule is complete. Measles supplementary dose was administered.
5.1.169 Decision Table Output Measles primary series is complete
5.1.170 Decision Table Output Measles primary series is complete. Two measles primary series doses were administered.
5.1.171 Decision Table Output Measles vaccination contraindicated
5.1.172 Decision Table Output Measles vaccination could be contraindicated. Clinical judgement required. Create a clinical note
5.1.173 Decision Table Output Member States should update this action according to the national immunization programme.
5.1.174 Decision Table Output Member States should update this guidance according to the national immunization programme.
5.1.175 Decision Table Output Members States should update this action according to the national immunization programme
5.1.176 Decision Table Output Meningococcal immunization schedule is complete
5.1.177 Decision Table Output Meningococcal immunization schedule is complete as clients age was more than 11 months when primary series was started. One primary series dose was administered. Check for any other vaccines due.
5.1.178 Decision Table Output Meningococcal immunization schedule is complete as clients age was more than 23 months when primary series was started. One primary series dose was administered.
5.1.179 Decision Table Output Meningococcal immunization schedule is complete. One meningococcal primary series dose was administered.
5.1.180 Decision Table Output Meningococcal immunization schedule is complete. One primary series dose and one booster dose have been administered.
5.1.181 Decision Table Output Meningococcal immunization schedule is complete. One primary series dose was administered.
5.1.182 Decision Table Output Meningococcal immunization schedule is complete. Two meningococcal primary series doses were administered.
5.1.183 Decision Table Output Meningococcal immunization schedule is complete. Two primary series doses and one booster dose were administered.
5.1.184 Decision Table Output Meningococcal immunization schedule is complete. Two primary series doses were administered.
5.1.185 Decision Table Output Mumps immunization schedule is complete
5.1.186 Decision Table Output Mumps immunization schedule is complete. Two mumps primary series doses were administered.
5.1.187 Decision Table Output Mumps vaccination could be contraindicated. Clinical judgement is required.
5.1.188 Decision Table Output Mumps vaccination is contraindicated
5.1.189 Decision Table Output Pertussis immunization schedule is complete
5.1.190 Decision Table Output Pertussis immunization schedule is complete. Three DTP primary series doses and one pertussis booster dose were administered.
5.1.191 Decision Table Output Pertussis immunization schedule is complete. Three DTP primary series doses were administered.
5.1.192 Decision Table Output Pneumococcal immunization schedule is complete
5.1.193 Decision Table Output Pneumococcal immunization schedule is complete. One pneumococcal dose was administered.
5.1.194 Decision Table Output Pneumococcal immunization schedule is complete. One primary series dose was administered.
5.1.195 Decision Table Output Pneumococcal immunization schedule is complete. Three pneumococcal primary serie doses were administered.
5.1.196 Decision Table Output Pneumococcal immunization schedule is complete. Three pneumococcal primary series doses and one booster dose were administered.
5.1.197 Decision Table Output Pneumococcal immunization schedule is complete. Three pneumococcal primary series doses were administered.
5.1.198 Decision Table Output Pneumococcal immunization schedule is complete. Two pneumococcal pneumococcal doses were administered.
5.1.199 Decision Table Output Pneumococcal immunization schedule is complete. Two pneumococcal primary series doses and one booster dose were administered.
5.1.200 Decision Table Output Pneumococcal immunization schedule is complete. Two pneumococcal primary series doses and two booster doses were administered.
5.1.201 Decision Table Output Pneumococcal immunization schedule is complete. Two pneumococcal primary series doses were administered.
5.1.202 Decision Table Output Polio immunization schedule is complete
5.1.203 Decision Table Output Polio immunization schedule is complete as clients age was more than 6 weeks when the primary series was started. Three poliovirus primary series doses were administered.
5.1.204 Decision Table Output Polio immunization schedule is complete. Three poliovirus primary series doses and a booster dose were administered.
5.1.205 Decision Table Output Polio routine immunization schedule is complete. Five poliovirus primary series doses were administered.
5.1.206 Decision Table Output Poliovirus birth dose was already administered. Check poliovirus immunization schedule.
5.1.207 Decision Table Output Poliovirus primary series is complete. Assess if supplementary bOPV dose(s) are necessary and create a clinical note.
5.1.208 Decision Table Output Poliovirus vaccination with bOPV is contraindicated
5.1.209 Decision Table Output Prescribe the Ty21a booster series. Should vaccinate client with typhoid booster dose as primary series has been completed, the latest typhoid dose was administered more than 3 years ago and no live vaccine was administered in the past 4 weeks.
5.1.210 Decision Table Output Prescribe the Ty21a primary series (if not done yet). Should vaccinate client with typhoid dose as primary series has not been completed and no live vaccines was administered in the past 4 weeks.
5.1.211 Decision Table Output Primary DTP series is complete. Client is due for pertussis booster dose
5.1.212 Decision Table Output Primary DTP series is complete. Client is due for tetanus and diphtheria booster dose
5.1.213 Decision Table Output Primary DTP series is complete. Client is not due for pertussis booster dose
5.1.214 Decision Table Output Primary DTP series is complete. Client is not due for tetanus and diphtheria booster dose
5.1.215 Decision Table Output Primary series is complete
5.1.216 Decision Table Output Primary series is complete. Client is due for cholera booster dose
5.1.217 Decision Table Output Primary series is complete. Client is due for Hib booster dose
5.1.218 Decision Table Output Primary series is complete. Client is due for IPV booster dose
5.1.219 Decision Table Output Primary series is complete. Client is due for TBE booster dose
5.1.220 Decision Table Output Primary series is complete. Client is due for typhoid booster dose
5.1.221 Decision Table Output Primary series is complete. Client is not due for cholera booster dose
5.1.222 Decision Table Output Primary series is complete. Client is not due for Hib booster dose
5.1.223 Decision Table Output Primary series is complete. Client is not due for IPV booster dose
5.1.224 Decision Table Output Primary series is complete. Client is not due for TBE booster dose
5.1.225 Decision Table Output Primary series is complete. Client is not due for typhoid booster dose
5.1.226 Decision Table Output Rabies immunization schedule is complete
5.1.227 Decision Table Output Rabies immunization schedule is complete. Two rabies primary series doses were administered.
5.1.228 Decision Table Output Recommend the client to perform TB infection testing.
5.1.229 Decision Table Output Rotavirus immunization schedule is complete
5.1.230 Decision Table Output Rotavirus immunization schedule is complete. Three rotavirus primary series doses were administered. Check for any other vaccines due.
5.1.231 Decision Table Output Rotavirus immunization schedule is complete. Two rotavirus doses were administered. Check for any other vaccines due.
5.1.232 Decision Table Output Rotavirus vaccination could be contraindicated. Clinical judgement is required. Create a clinical note
5.1.233 Decision Table Output Rotavirus vaccination is contraindicated
5.1.234 Decision Table Output Rubella immunization schedule is complete
5.1.235 Decision Table Output Rubella immunization schedule is complete. One rubella primary series dose was administered.
5.1.236 Decision Table Output Rubella vaccination could be contraindicated. Clinical judgement is required. Create a clinical note
5.1.237 Decision Table Output Rubella vaccination is contraindicated
5.1.238 Decision Table Output Seasonal influenza vaccination could be contraindicated. Clinical judgement is required. Create a clinical note
5.1.239 Decision Table Output Shold not vaccinate client with first IPV dose as clients age is less than 6 weeks.
5.1.240 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client as clients age is less than 9 months.
5.1.241 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with annual dose as latest seasonal influenza dose was administered less than 1 year ago.
5.1.242 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with annual dose as the latest seasonal influenza dose was administered less than 1 year ago.
5.1.243 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with bOPV birth dose (a zero dose) as clients age is more than or equal to 1 week. Check for any other vaccines due and inform the caregiver of when to come back for the next dose.
5.1.244 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with cholera booster dose as primary series was completed less than 3 years ago.
5.1.245 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with cholera booster dose as the latest dose was administered less than 14 days ago.
5.1.246 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with cholera dose as clients age is less than 2 years.
5.1.247 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with cholera dose as clients age is under 1 year.
5.1.248 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with cholera dose as the booster series for cholera was completed less than 3 years ago.
5.1.249 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with cholera dose as the latest cholera dose was administered less than 1 week ago.
5.1.250 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with cholera dose as the latest cholera dose was administered less than 14 days ago.
5.1.251 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with cholera dose as the latest cholera dose was administered less than {Member States defined lower limit} days ago.
5.1.252 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first BCG dose as ART has not been started.
5.1.253 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first BCG dose as client is not clinically well.
5.1.254 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first BCG dose as client is not currently receiving ART.
5.1.255 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first BCG dose as client is not immnologically stable.
5.1.256 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first BCG dose as client is not immunologically stable.
5.1.257 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first BCG dose as clients TB infection test result is positive. Consider evaluating for TB disease or for TB preventive treatment (TPT) eligibility (once TB disease is ruled out).
5.1.258 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first BCG dose as live vaccine was administered in the last 4 weeks.
5.1.259 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first BCG dose as live vaccine was administered in the past 4 weeks.
5.1.260 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first bOPV dose as clients age is less than 6 weeks.
5.1.261 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first dengue dose as client does not have evidence of past dengue infection.
5.1.262 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first dengue dose as clients age is less than 9 years.
5.1.263 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first dengue dose as clients age is more than 45 years.
5.1.264 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first DTP dose as client is less than 6 weeks old.
5.1.265 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first hepatitis A dose as clients age is less than 12 months.
5.1.266 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first hepatitis B dose as clients age is not within appropriate age range.
5.1.267 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first Hib dose as client is less than 6 weeks old.
5.1.268 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first Hib dose as clients age is less than 6 weeks.
5.1.269 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first HPV dose as clients age is less than 9 years.
5.1.270 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first IPV dose as clients age is less than 8 weeks.
5.1.271 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first JE dose as clients age is under 6 months.
5.1.272 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first malaria dose as clients age is less than 5 months.
5.1.273 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first meningococcal dose as clients age is less than 2 years.
5.1.274 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first meningococcal dose as clients age is less than 2 years. Check for any other vaccines due and inform the caregiver of when to come back for the first dose.
5.1.275 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first meningococcal dose as clients age is less than 9 months.
5.1.276 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first meningococcal dose as clients age is under 2 months. Check for any vaccines due and inform the caregiver of when to come back for the first dose.
5.1.277 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first meningococcal dose as clients age is under 3 months.
5.1.278 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first mumps dose as clients age is less than 12 months.
5.1.279 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first mumps dose as live vaccine was administered in the past 4 weeks.
5.1.280 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first pneumococcal dose as clients age is less than 6 weeks.
5.1.281 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first pneumococcal dose as clients age is less than 6 weeks. Check for any vaccines due and inform the caregiver of when to come back for the first dose.
5.1.282 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first rotavirus dose as clients age is less than 6 weeks. Check for any other vaccines due and inform the caregiver of when to come back for the first dose.
5.1.283 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first rotavirus dose as live vaccine was administered in last 4 weeks. Check for any vaccines due and inform the caregiver of when to come back for the first dose.
5.1.284 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first seasonal influenza dose as clients age is less than 6 months.
5.1.285 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first TBE dose as clients age is less than 1 year.
5.1.286 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first TBE dose as clients age is less than 3 years.
5.1.287 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first TBE dose as clients age is under 3 years.
5.1.288 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first tetanus and diphtheria booster dose as clients age is less than 12 months.
5.1.289 Decision Table Output Should not vaccinate client with first tetanus and diphtheria booster dose as the latest DTP dose was administered less than 1 year ago.

5.1.500 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with second meningococcal dose as the latest meningococcal dose was administered more than 8 weeks ago.
5.1.501 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with second mumps dose as latest mumps dose was administered more than 4 weeks ago and no live vaccine was administered in the past 4 weeks.
5.1.502 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with second pneumococcal booster dose as client had preterm birth and latest pneumococcal dose was administered within 12 months post birth.
5.1.503 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with second pneumococcal booster dose as client is HIV positive and latest pneumococcal dose was administered within 12 months post birth.
5.1.504 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with second pneumococcal dose as first dose was administered 24 months post birth, latest pneumococcal dose was administered more than 8 weeks ago and client is at high risk for pneumococcal infection.
5.1.505 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with second pneumococcal dose as first dose was administered within 12 months post birth and latest pneumococcal dose was administered more than 4 weeks ago.
5.1.506 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with second pneumococcal dose as the latest pneumococcal dose was administered more than 4 weeks ago and client is not at high risk for pneumococcal infection.
5.1.507 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with second pneumococcal dose as the latest pneumococcal dose was administered more than 8 weeks ago and client is at high risk for pneumococcal infection.
5.1.508 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with second pneumococcal dose as the latest pneumococcal dose was administered more than 8 weeks ago.
5.1.509 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with second rabies dose as the latest rabies dose was administerd 7 or more days ago.
5.1.510 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with second rotavirus dose as clients age is within appropriate age range and no live vaccine was administered in the last 4 weeks.
5.1.511 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with second seasonal influenza dose as client is within appropriate age range and latest seasonal influenza dose was administerered more than 4 weeks ago.
5.1.512 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with second TBE dose as the latest dose was administered more than 1 month ago.
5.1.513 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with second TBE dose as the latest dose was administered more than 5 months ago.
5.1.514 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with second tetanus and diphtheria booster dose as client is currently pregnant and the latest tetanus and diphtheria dose was administered more than 4 weeks ago.
5.1.515 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with second tetanus and diphtheria booster dose as client is within appropriate age range and the latest tetanus and diphtheria booster dose was administered more than 4 years ago.
5.1.516 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with second tetanus and diphtheria booster dose as the latest tetanus and diphtheria dose was administered more than 1 year ago.
5.1.517 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with second tetanus and diphtheria booster dose as three DTP primary series doses and one tetanus and diphtheria booster dose were administered and client is currently pregnant.
5.1.518 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with second varicella dose as the latest varicella dose was administered more than 4 weeks ago and no live vaccine was administered in past 4 weeks. Check for contraindications.
5.1.519 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with TBE booster dose as the latest TBE dose was administered more than 3 years ago.
5.1.520 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with TBE booster dose as the primary series has been completed and the latest TBE dose was administered more than 3 years ago.
5.1.521 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with third bOPV dose and second IPV dose as two bOPV doses and one IPV dose were administered, the latest bOPV dose was administered more than 4 weeks ago and the latest IPV dose was administered more than 4 months ago.
5.1.522 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with third bOPV dose as clients age is between 6 and 14 weeks and the latest bOPV dose was administered more than 4 weeks ago. Check for contraindications.
5.1.523 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with third bOPV dose as two bOPV doses and one IPV dose were administered, the latest bOPV dose was administered more than 4 weeks ago and the latest IPV dose was administered less than 4 months ago.
5.1.524 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with third bOPV dose as two bOPV doses and two IPV doses were administered and the latest bOPV dose was administered more than 4 weeks ago.
5.1.525 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with third dengue dose as latest dengue dose was administered more than 6 months ago and client has evidence of past dengue infection.
5.1.526 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with third dengue dose as latest dengue dose was administered more than 6 months ago.
5.1.527 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with third DTP dose as the latest DTP dose was administered more than 4 weeks ago.
5.1.528 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with third DTP dose as the latest DTP dose was administered more than 6 months ago.
5.1.529 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with third hepatitis B dose as the client was a low-birth-weight infant and the latest hepatitis B dose was administered more than 4 weeks ago.
5.1.530 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with third hepatitis B dose as the client was a low-birth-weight infant and the latest hepatitis B-containing vaccine administered more than 4 weeks ago.
5.1.531 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with third hepatitis B dose as the first hepatitis B dose was administered more than 6 months ago and the latest hepatitis B dose was administered more than 4 weeks ago.
5.1.532 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with third hepatitis B dose as the latest Hepatitis B dose was administered more than 4 weeks ago.
5.1.533 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with third Hib dose as client is within appropriate age range and latest Hib dose was administered more than 4 weeks ago.
5.1.534 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with third Hib dose as client is within appropriate age range and the latest Hib dose was administered more than 4 weeks ago.
5.1.535 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with third HPV dose as client is HIV positive and the latest HPV dose was administered more than 6 months ago.
5.1.536 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with third HPV dose as client is immunocompromised and the latest HPV dose was administered more than 6 months ago.
5.1.537 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with third IPV dose as the latest poliovirus dose was administered more than 4 weeks ago.
5.1.538 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with third malaria dose as the latest malaria dose was administered more than 4 weeks ago.
5.1.539 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with third pneumococcal dose as clients age is less than 5 yers and latest pneumococcal dose was administered more than 4 weeks ago.
5.1.540 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with third rotavirus dose as no live vaccine was administered in the last 4 weeks and series administered to client is a mixed or unknown series.
5.1.541 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with third rotavirus dose as series contains more than two doses and no live vaccine was administered in the last 4 weeks.
5.1.542 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with third TBE dose as the latest dose was administered more than 12 months ago.
5.1.543 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with third TBE dose as the latest dose was administered more than 5 months ago.
5.1.544 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with third TBE dose as the latest dose was administered more than 9 months ago.
5.1.545 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with third tetanus and diphtheria booster dose as client is within appropriate age range and the latest tetanus and diphtheria dose was administered more than 4 years ago.
5.1.546 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with third tetanus and diphtheria booster dose as the latest tetanus and diphtheria dose was administered more than 1 year ago. This dose should be administered at least 1 year after second booster dose or durin7158e3e2fd
5.1.547 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with typhoid booster dose as the latest dose was administered more than 3 years ago.
5.1.548 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with typhoid dose as no typhoid doses were administered and client is within appropriate age range.
5.1.549 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with varicella dose as no varicella dose was administered, client is within the appropriate age range and no live vaccine was administered in past 4 weeks.
5.1.550 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate client with yellow fever dose as no yellow fever dose was administered, client is within appropriate age range and no live vaccine was administered in the past 4 weeks.
5.1.551 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate for third HPV dose as client is HIV positive and the latest HPV dose was administered more than 6 months ago.
5.1.552 Decision Table Output Should vaccinate for third HPV dose as client is immunocompromised and the latest HPV dose was administered more than 6 months ago.
5.1.553 Decision Table Output Tetanus and diphtheria immunization schedule is complete
5.1.554 Decision Table Output Tetanus and diphtheria immunization schedule is complete. Three DTP primary series doses and three tetanus and diphtheria booster doses were administered.
5.1.555 Decision Table Output Tetanus and diphtheria immunization schedule is complete. Three DTP primary series doses and two tetanus and diphtheria booster doses were administered.
5.1.556 Decision Table Output Tetanus and diphtheria immunization schedule is complete.Three DTP primary series doses and three tetanus and diphtheria booster doses were administered. There is no need for any additional tetanus and diphtheria doses.
5.1.557 Decision Table Output Tetanus and diphtheria immunization schedule is complete.Three DTP primary series doses and two tetanus and diphtheria booster doses were administered. There is no need for any additional tetanus and diphtheria doses.
5.1.558 Decision Table Output Tetanus vaccination is contraindicated
5.1.559 Decision Table Output The client has a delayed start. Please follow the delayed or interrupted schedule.
5.1.560 Decision Table Output The client has a delayed start. Please follow the delayed start schedule.
5.1.561 Decision Table Output The client has received 3 tetanus-contzining vaccine doses during childhood. Please follow the pregnancy starting with 3 doses schedule.
5.1.562 Decision Table Output The client has received 4 tetanus-containing vaccines doses during childhood or pre-adulthood. Please follow the pregnancy starting with 4 doses schedule.
5.1.563 Decision Table Output The client is currently pregnant, consider risks of vaccination and make a judgement. Inactivated Vero cell-derived vaccines are preferred over live attenuated or live recombinant vaccines.
5.1.564 Decision Table Output The hepatitis B immunization schedule is complete for client whose weight at birth was below 2000 g and who was a premature infant. Four hepatitis B primary series doses were administered.
5.1.565 Decision Table Output The hepatitis B immunization schedule is complete. Three hepatitis B primary series doses were administered.
5.1.566 Decision Table Output The primary series is complete
5.1.567 Decision Table Output The primary series is complete. Client is due for meningococcal booster dose
5.1.568 Decision Table Output The primary series is complete. Client is due for seasonal influenza annual dose
5.1.569 Decision Table Output The primary series is complete. Client is not due for meningococcal booster dose.
5.1.570 Decision Table Output The primary series is complete. Client is not due for seasonal influenza annual dose
5.1.571 Decision Table Output The primary series is complete. Client is not due for TBE booster dose
5.1.572 Decision Table Output The primary series is complete. Clinical judgement is required for meningococcal booster dose. Create a clinical note
5.1.573 Decision Table Output Typhoid immunization schedule is complete
5.1.574 Decision Table Output Typhoid immunization schedule is complete. One typhoid primary series dose was administered.
5.1.575 Decision Table Output Varicella immunization schedule is complete
5.1.576 Decision Table Output Varicella immunization schedule is complete. One varicella primary series dose was administered.
5.1.577 Decision Table Output Varicella immunization schedule is complete. Two varicella primary series doses were administered. Check for any other vaccines due.
5.1.578 Decision Table Output Varicella vaccination could be contraindicated. Clinical judgement is required. Create clinical note
5.1.579 Decision Table Output Varicella vaccination is usually contraindicated in clients with congenital or acquired immune deficiencies. Consider risks of vaccination and make a judgement.
5.1.580 Decision Table Output Yellow fever immunization schedule is complete
5.1.581 Decision Table Output Yellow fever immunization schedule is complete. One yellow fever primary series dose was administered.
